title: “Stealth Crawlers” Are Not a Threat to the Open Web. Bills Targeting Them Would Be.
excerpt: There’s a new boogeyman in the battles over AI: so-called “stealth crawlers.” We’ll admit it—the term “stealth crawlers” sounds quite nefarious. In reality, they’re anything but.“Stealth crawlers” are simply automated tools to access and collect public web data—without disclosing the user’s...
content_html:
There’s a new boogeyman in the battles over AI: so-called “stealth crawlers.” We’ll admit it—the term “stealth crawlers” sounds quite nefarious. In reality, they’re anything but.

“Stealth crawlers” are simply automated tools to access and collect public web data—without disclosing the user’s identity. Private crawlers like these facilitate all kinds of important work that benefits the public, including investigative reporting, academic research, cybersecurity protection, and more.

Anonymous crawling enables some of the most publicly beneficial uses of the open web.

Many publishers want to unmask crawlers anyways—and are pushing for new legislation that would give them new powers to do so. These legislative proposals threaten the open web, user privacy, and valuable research without directly addressing the problems they’re supposedly intending to solve.

Alarmingly, these harmful proposals are gaining traction. The New York state legislature has already passed such a bill, the NY Stealth Crawler Protection Act, which is now on Governor Hochul’s desk. We expect to see similar bills introduced in other states, and potentially in Congress. That’s a big problem for the open web—and the many benefits it provides.

Anonymous crawling is worth protecting

Anonymous crawling enables some of the most publicly beneficial uses of the open web. Researchers, journalists, and other watchdog groups use unidentified automated tools to gather the information necessary to hold powerful institutions accountable and protect the public.

Anonymous crawling fuels important investigative journalism. For example, The Markup, a non-profit news site, used anonymous crawlers to investigate potentially anti-competitive practices by tech companies, such as Amazon’s tendency to prioritize Amazon brands and Amazon-exclusive products over competitors with higher ratings. The crawlers identified themselves as ordinary Firefox browsers to web servers, which allowed The Markup to understand how Amazon search results pages would appear to ordinary users. Similarly, ProPublica used an automated tool designed to simulate an ordinary Amazon customer to reveal that the site steered shoppers to more expensive products over cheaper alternatives.

Anonymous web scraping is also crucial for cybersecurity professionals, who use automated tools to monitor the web for information that helps them protect against malicious attackers. Privacy tools, including EFF’s own Privacy Badger, also crawl sites anonymously to identify trackers without compromising user privacy.

However, without the ability to scrape anonymously, these tools would likely be blocked. Sites can—and do—block crawlers operated by researchers, journalists, and activists who criticize them. For example, Facebook shut down accounts belonging to researchers who used automated tools to study misinformation on the platform and demanded that they take down published research. Many sites block automated access by anyone who hasn’t paid to crawl public webpages.    

Unmasking crawlers threatens the open web

News publishers—and their allies in government—say that unmasking crawlers is necessary to protect news organizations from technological strain caused by AI-related crawling, and fears that AI could reduce news sites’ traffic and ad revenue. These are legitimate concerns.

But enacting broad, reactionary restrictions on automated access is not the answer. Legislation targeting anonymous crawling threatens the open web, user privacy, and valuable research without actually addressing these technological and potential economic harms of scraping.

The New York state legislature recently passed the NY Stealth Crawler Protection Act, a law that would make it illegal to crawl news websites without revealing who is operating the crawler and all possible future uses of the data collected by the crawler. The law would give websites the power to obtain court orders that unmask anyone using an unidentified crawler—without any evidence that they broke the law.

Laws like the New York bill sweep far beyond AI, and do not meaningfully address the technological or potential harms of AI-related web scraping. These policies would chill beneficial crawling by allowing publishers to veto lawful public access, giving them the power to block not just bad actors, but also security professionals, researchers, dissidents, or anyone who has not paid for a license to view public text. This needlessly undermines the free and open internet.

Digital news publishers—like most websites—face real technological challenges in the AI era. While web crawling has been around for decades, with the proliferation of AI, crawlers now collect far more public web data than they used to. This pushes servers closer to their maximum capacity, and if some bots collect information too aggressively, they may strain web servers to the point that it degrades site performance. The problem is not anonymity—so unmasking crawlers won’t solve it. The real problem is overaggressive crawling, which can be effectively addressed with technical measures that target harmful conduct without impeding anonymous access to information.

A better path forward

There are other, far less harmful ways to protect publishers from the harms these “stealth crawler” laws claim to target. Addressing the harms of AI-related crawling requires policies that narrowly target the causes of these issues–without undermining free expression and the open web. Policies that target crawlers and scrapers are anything but.

sentences.en: Anonymous crawling fuels important investigative journalism.
sentences.zh: 匿名爬行推动了重要的调查性新闻报道。

sentences.en: For example, The Markup, a non-profit news site, used anonymous crawlers to investigate potentially anti-competitive practices by tech companies, such as Amazon’s tendency to prioritize Amazon brands and Amazon-exclusive products over competitors with higher ratings.
sentences.zh: 例如，非营利新闻网站 The Markup 使用匿名爬虫来调查科技公司潜在的反竞争行为，例如亚马逊倾向于优先考虑亚马逊品牌和亚马逊独家产品，而不是评级较高的竞争对手。

sentences.en: The crawlers identified themselves as ordinary Firefox browsers to web servers, which allowed The Markup to understand how Amazon search results pages would appear to ordinary users.
sentences.zh: 这些爬虫将自己标识为网络服务器的普通 Firefox 浏览器，这使得 The Markup 能够了解亚马逊搜索结果页面对普通用户的显示方式。

sentences.en: Similarly, ProPublica used an automated tool designed to simulate an ordinary Amazon customer to reveal that the site steered shoppers to more expensive products over cheaper alternatives.
sentences.zh: 同样，ProPublica 使用一种旨在模拟普通亚马逊客户的自动化工具来揭示该网站引导购物者购买更昂贵的产品而不是更便宜的替代品。

blocks.html_en: Anonymous web scraping is also crucial for cybersecurity professionals, who use automated tools to monitor the web for information that helps them protect against malicious attackers. Privacy tools, including EFF’s own Privacy Badger, also crawl sites anonymously to identify trackers without compromising user privacy.

sentences.en: Anonymous web scraping is also crucial for cybersecurity professionals, who use automated tools to monitor the web for information that helps them protect against malicious attackers.

sentences.en: Privacy tools, including EFF’s own Privacy Badger, also crawl sites anonymously to identify trackers without compromising user privacy.
sentences.zh: 隐私工具，包括 EFF 自己的 Privacy Badger，也可以匿名抓取网站以识别跟踪器，而不会损害用户隐私。

blocks.html_en: However, without the ability to scrape anonymously, these tools would likely be blocked. Sites can—and do—block crawlers operated by researchers, journalists, and activists who criticize them. For example, Facebook shut down accounts belonging to researchers who used automated tools to study misinformation on the platform and demanded that they take down published research. Many sites block automated access by anyone who hasn’t paid to crawl public webpages.

sentences.en: However, without the ability to scrape anonymously, these tools would likely be blocked.
sentences.zh: 然而，如果没有匿名抓取的能力，这些工具可能会被阻止。

sentences.en: Sites can—and do—block crawlers operated by researchers, journalists, and activists who criticize them.
sentences.zh: 网站可以而且确实阻止了由研究人员、记者和批评它们的活动人士操作的爬虫。

sentences.en: For example, Facebook shut down accounts belonging to researchers who used automated tools to study misinformation on the platform and demanded that they take down published research.
sentences.zh: 例如，Facebook 关闭了使用自动化工具研究平台上错误信息的研究人员的账户，并要求他们撤下已发表的研究成果。

sentences.en: Many sites block automated access by anyone who hasn’t paid to crawl public webpages.
sentences.zh: 许多网站会阻止任何未付费抓取公共网页的人进行自动访问。

sentences.en: Unmasking crawlers threatens the open web
sentences.zh: 揭露爬虫威胁开放网络

blocks.html_en: News publishers—and their allies in government—say that unmasking crawlers is necessary to protect news organizations from technological strain caused by AI-related crawling, and fears that AI could reduce news sites’ traffic and ad revenue. These are legitimate concerns.

sentences.en: News publishers—and their allies in government—say that unmasking crawlers is necessary to protect news organizations from technological strain caused by AI-related crawling, and fears that AI could reduce news sites’ traffic and ad revenue.
sentences.zh: 新闻出版商及其在政府中的盟友表示，揭露爬虫对于保护新闻机构免受人工智能相关爬行造成的技术压力是必要的，并担心人工智能可能会减少新闻网站的流量和广告收入。

sentences.en: These are legitimate concerns.
sentences.zh: 这些都是合理的担忧。

blocks.html_en: But enacting broad, reactionary restrictions on automated access is not the answer. Legislation targeting anonymous crawling threatens the open web, user privacy, and valuable research without actually addressing these technological and potential economic harms of scraping.

sentences.en: But enacting broad, reactionary restrictions on automated access is not the answer.
sentences.zh: 但对自动访问实施广泛的、反动的限制并不是解决办法。

sentences.en: Legislation targeting anonymous crawling threatens the open web, user privacy, and valuable research without actually addressing these technological and potential economic harms of scraping.
sentences.zh: 针对匿名爬行的立法威胁着开放网络、用户隐私和有价值的研究，而没有真正解决爬行的这些技术和潜在经济危害。

blocks.html_en: The New York state legislature recently passed the NY Stealth Crawler Protection Act, a law that would make it illegal to crawl news websites without revealing who is operating the crawler and all possible future uses of the data collected by the crawler. The law would give websites the power to obtain court orders that unmask anyone using an unidentified crawler—without any evidence that they broke the law.

sentences.en: The New York state legislature recently passed the NY Stealth Crawler Protection Act, a law that would make it illegal to crawl news websites without revealing who is operating the crawler and all possible future uses of the data collected by the crawler.
sentences.zh: 纽约州立法机构最近通过了《纽约隐形爬虫保护法案》，该法律规定，在不透露爬虫操作者以及爬虫收集的数据的所有未来可能用途的情况下爬取新闻网站属于非法行为。

sentences.en: The law would give websites the power to obtain court orders that unmask anyone using an unidentified crawler—without any evidence that they broke the law.
sentences.zh: 该法律将赋予网站获得法院命令的权力，揭露任何使用身份不明爬虫的人，而无需任何证据表明他们违反了法律。

blocks.html_en: Laws like the New York bill sweep far beyond AI, and do not meaningfully address the technological or potential harms of AI-related web scraping. These policies would chill beneficial crawling by allowing publishers to veto lawful public access, giving them the power to block not just bad actors, but also security professionals, researchers, dissidents, or anyone who has not paid for a license to view public text. This needlessly undermines the free and open internet.

sentences.en: Laws like the New York bill sweep far beyond AI, and do not meaningfully address the technological or potential harms of AI-related web scraping.
sentences.zh: 像纽约法案这样的法律所涵盖的范围远远超出了人工智能的范围，并且没有有意义地解决与人工智能相关的网络抓取的技术或潜在危害。

sentences.en: These policies would chill beneficial crawling by allowing publishers to veto lawful public access, giving them the power to block not just bad actors, but also security professionals, researchers, dissidents, or anyone who has not paid for a license to view public text.
sentences.zh: 这些政策将允许出版商否决合法的公共访问，从而阻止有益的爬行，使他们不仅有能力阻止不良行为者，还可以阻止安全专业人员、研究人员、持不同政见者或任何未付费获得查看公共文本许可的人。

sentences.en: This needlessly undermines the free and open internet.
sentences.zh: 这不必要地破坏了自由开放的互联网。

blocks.html_en: Digital news publishers—like most websites—face real technological challenges in the AI era. While web crawling has been around for decades, with the proliferation of AI, crawlers now collect far more public web data than they used to. This pushes servers closer to their maximum capacity, and if some bots collect information too aggressively, they may strain web servers to the point that it degrades site performance. The problem is not anonymity—so unmasking crawlers won’t solve it. The real problem is overaggressive crawling, which can be effectively addressed with technical measures that target harmful conduct without impeding anonymous access to information.

sentences.en: Digital news publishers—like most websites—face real technological challenges in the AI era.
sentences.zh: 与大多数网站一样，数字新闻出版商在人工智能时代面临着真正的技术挑战。

sentences.en: While web crawling has been around for decades, with the proliferation of AI, crawlers now collect far more public web data than they used to.
sentences.zh: 虽然网络爬行已经存在了几十年，但随着人工智能的普及，爬虫现在收集的公共网络数据比以前多得多。

sentences.en: This pushes servers closer to their maximum capacity, and if some bots collect information too aggressively, they may strain web servers to the point that it degrades site performance.
sentences.zh: 这使得服务器更接近其最大容量，如果某些机器人过于积极地收集信息，它们可能会给 Web 服务器带来压力，从而降低站点性能。

sentences.en: The problem is not anonymity—so unmasking crawlers won’t solve it.
sentences.zh: 问题不在于匿名——因此揭露爬虫程序并不能解决问题。

sentences.en: The real problem is overaggressive crawling, which can be effectively addressed with technical measures that target harmful conduct without impeding anonymous access to information.
sentences.zh: 真正的问题是过度攻击性的爬行，可以通过针对有害行为的技术措施来有效解决这个问题，同时又不妨碍匿名访问信息。

sentences.en: A better path forward
sentences.zh: 更好的前进道路

blocks.html_en: There are other, far less harmful ways to protect publishers from the harms these “stealth crawler” laws claim to target. Addressing the harms of AI-related crawling requires policies that narrowly target the causes of these issues–without undermining free expression and the open web. Policies that target crawlers and scrapers are anything but.

sentences.en: There are other, far less harmful ways to protect publishers from the harms these “stealth crawler” laws claim to target.
sentences.zh: 还有其他危害较小的方法可以保护出版商免受这些“隐形爬虫”法律声称针对的伤害。

sentences.en: Addressing the harms of AI-related crawling requires policies that narrowly target the causes of these issues–without undermining free expression and the open web.
sentences.zh: 要解决与人工智能相关的爬行的危害，需要制定专门针对这些问题的原因的政策，同时又不损害言论自由和开放网络。

sentences.en: Policies that target crawlers and scrapers are anything but.
sentences.zh: 针对爬虫和爬虫的政策绝非如此。

content_html_zh:
人工智能之战中出现了一个新的怪物：所谓的“隐形爬虫”。 我们承认——“隐形爬虫”这个词听起来很邪恶。 事实上，它们绝非如此。

“隐形爬虫”只是访问和收集公共网络数据的自动化工具，而不会泄露用户的身份。 像这样的私人爬虫促进了各种有益于公众的重要工作，包括调查报告、学术研究、网络安全保护等。

匿名爬行可以实现开放网络的一些最公共有益的用途。

无论如何，许多出版商都想揭开爬虫的面纱，并正在推动新的立法，赋予他们这样做的新权力。 这些立法提案威胁了开放网络、用户隐私和有价值的研究，而没有直接解决他们本应解决的问题。

令人担忧的是，这些有害的提议正在获得关注。 纽约州立法机关已经通过了这样一项法案，即《纽约隐形爬行者保护法案》，该法案现已放在州长霍赫尔的办公桌上。 我们预计其他州也会提出类似的法案，甚至可能会在国会提出。 对于开放网络及其提供的诸多好处来说，这是一个大问题。

匿名爬行值得保护

匿名爬行可以实现开放网络的一些最公共有益的用途。 研究人员、记者和其他监督团体使用身份不明的自动化工具来收集必要的信息，以追究强大机构的责任并保护公众。

匿名爬行推动了重要的调查性新闻报道。 例如，非营利新闻网站 The Markup 使用匿名爬虫来调查科技公司潜在的反竞争行为，例如亚马逊倾向于优先考虑亚马逊品牌和亚马逊独家产品，而不是评级较高的竞争对手。 这些爬虫将自己标识为网络服务器的普通 Firefox 浏览器，这使得 The Markup 能够了解亚马逊搜索结果页面对普通用户的显示方式。 同样，ProPublica 使用一种旨在模拟普通亚马逊客户的自动化工具来揭示该网站引导购物者购买更昂贵的产品而不是更便宜的替代品。

匿名网络抓取对于网络安全专业人员来说也至关重要，他们使用自动化工具来监控网络中的信息，以帮助他们防范恶意攻击者。 隐私工具，包括 EFF 自己的 Privacy Badger，也可以匿名抓取网站以识别跟踪器，而不会损害用户隐私。

然而，如果没有匿名抓取的能力，这些工具可能会被阻止。 网站可以而且确实阻止了由研究人员、记者和批评它们的活动人士操作的爬虫。 例如，Facebook 关闭了使用自动化工具研究平台上错误信息的研究人员的账户，并要求他们撤下已发表的研究成果。 许多网站会阻止任何未付费抓取公共网页的人进行自动访问。

揭露爬虫威胁开放网络

新闻出版商及其在政府中的盟友表示，揭露爬虫对于保护新闻机构免受人工智能相关爬行造成的技术压力是必要的，并担心人工智能可能会减少新闻网站的流量和广告收入。 这些都是合理的担忧。

但对自动访问实施广泛的、反动的限制并不是解决办法。 针对匿名爬行的立法威胁着开放网络、用户隐私和有价值的研究，而没有真正解决爬行的这些技术和潜在经济危害。

纽约州立法机构最近通过了《纽约隐形爬虫保护法案》，该法律规定，在不透露爬虫操作者以及爬虫收集的数据的所有未来可能用途的情况下爬取新闻网站属于非法行为。 该法律将赋予网站获得法院命令的权力，揭露任何使用身份不明爬虫的人，而无需任何证据表明他们违反了法律。

像纽约法案这样的法律所涵盖的范围远远超出了人工智能的范围，并且没有有意义地解决与人工智能相关的网络抓取的技术或潜在危害。 这些政策将允许出版商否决合法的公共访问，从而阻止有益的爬行，使他们不仅有能力阻止不良行为者，还可以阻止安全专业人员、研究人员、持不同政见者或任何未付费获得查看公共文本许可的人。 这不必要地破坏了自由开放的互联网。

与大多数网站一样，数字新闻出版商在人工智能时代面临着真正的技术挑战。 虽然网络爬行已经存在了几十年，但随着人工智能的普及，爬虫现在收集的公共网络数据比以前多得多。 这使得服务器更接近其最大容量，如果某些机器人过于积极地收集信息，它们可能会给 Web 服务器带来压力，从而降低站点性能。 问题不在于匿名——因此揭露爬虫程序并不能解决问题。 真正的问题是过度攻击性的爬行，可以通过针对有害行为的技术措施来有效解决这个问题，同时又不妨碍匿名访问信息。

更好的前进道路

还有其他危害较小的方法可以保护出版商免受这些“隐形爬虫”法律声称针对的伤害。 要解决与人工智能相关的爬行的危害，需要制定专门针对这些问题的原因的政策，同时又不损害言论自由和开放网络。 针对爬虫和爬虫的政策绝非如此。
